summary: Descriptive overview of unconscionability in substantive law: procedural vs substantive, adhesion contracts.
key_points: procedural vs substantive is a fundamental concept in contract law.
Legal standards for unconscionability are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine unconscionability in litigation.
The doctrine of unconscionability has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding unconscionability requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
